name: Governorate of Livonia (Russian Empire)

colors.symbolism: derived from the governorate's heraldic colours

description: A horizontal tricolour of red, green and white in equal bands.
history: The Governorate of Livonia (Liflandia) was a Baltic governorate of the Russian Empire from 1796 until 1918, centred on Riga and covering much of present-day Vidzeme (Latvia) and southern Estonia. Its provincial tricolour of red, green and white derived from the Baltic-German Livonian provincial colours.
